texto: People like to make up stories about things they don’t understand, so there are over a thousand myths about the moon. One legend claims that on the surface is everything that was wasted here on Earth: misspent time, squandered wealth, broken vows, unanswered prayers, useless tears, all the leftover bits and pieces of countless shattered lives.If you believe in that sort of thing.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Bob Thurber: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “People like to make up stories about things they don’t understand, so there are over a thousand m…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, moon, paperboy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e paperboy.

frase_original: People like to make up stories about things they don’t understand, so there are over a thousand myths about the moon. One legend claims that on the surface is everything that was wasted here on Earth: misspent time, squandered wealth, broken vows, unanswered prayers, useless tears, all the leftover bits and pieces of countless shattered lives.If you believe in that sort of thing.

texto: Throughout my entire miserable childhood I woke at least once a night weeping from overwhelming delight. I did it hungry, I did it after beatings, I did it after the deaths of loved ones.Now you tell me if I’m crazy or if I’ve always been blessed.
explicacao: Citação de Bob Thurber sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Throughout my entire miserable childhood I woke at least once a night weeping from overwhelming d…” articula familia, abuse-survivors, blessed em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e luto.

frase_original: Throughout my entire miserable childhood I woke at least once a night weeping from overwhelming delight. I did it hungry, I did it after beatings, I did it after the deaths of loved ones.Now you tell me if I’m crazy or if I’ve always been blessed.

texto: Over the years our mother has beaten us with belts, shoes, rulers, extension cords, hair brushes, a wooden spoon, a fly swatter, a toilet brush, wire coat hangers, wooden coat hangers and sometimes one of our own toys. When you get whacked by your own paddleball paddle or you have to watch your sister getting spanked with a badminton racquet that she asked Santa Claus (AKA Grandma) to bring, you don't feel much like playing with those things ever again.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Bob Thurber: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Over the years our mother has beaten us with belts, shoes, rulers, extension cords, hair brushes,…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, bad-mothering, child-abuse pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de child-abuse e childhood.

frase_original: Over the years our mother has beaten us with belts, shoes, rulers, extension cords, hair brushes, a wooden spoon, a fly swatter, a toilet brush, wire coat hangers, wooden coat hangers and sometimes one of our own toys. When you get whacked by your own paddleball paddle or you have to watch your sister getting spanked with a badminton racquet that she asked Santa Claus (AKA Grandma) to bring, you don't feel much like playing with those things ever again.

texto: Memory in the mind of man can adapt to the worst conditions. I'll give you an example, an analogy of sorts: Each night I sop rags with beer and lay them out in careful strips. With rags soaked in beer I tease cockroaches from a crack in the baseboard. By morning they're good and drunk and I pop them into a baggy, then take them outside and throw the little buggers away.
explicacao: Citação de Bob Thurber sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Memory in the mind of man can adapt to the worst conditions. I'll give you an example, an analogy…” articula reflexao, cockroaches, memory em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como bom-dia e boa-noite.

frase_original: Memory in the mind of man can adapt to the worst conditions. I'll give you an example, an analogy of sorts: Each night I sop rags with beer and lay them out in careful strips. With rags soaked in beer I tease cockroaches from a crack in the baseboard. By morning they're good and drunk and I pop them into a baggy, then take them outside and throw the little buggers away.
